project_specification.project_name: Human-Vector Offline Navigation (Anumaan) + SDE via UDP Bridge
project_specification.overview: Build a zero-GPS, fully offline navigation state machine. The system uses time-based dead reckoning to predict arrival at OpenStreetMap (OSM) milestones. It implements a Stationary Detection Engine (SDE) using accelerometer data to auto-snap to nodes when stopped. Because the development environment (a laptop) lacks a physical IMU, the system MUST be architected with a decoupled sensor ingestion layer. It will receive real-time, live telemetry from a smartphone via a local UDP/HTTP bridge over a mobile hotspot.

boundaries.must_do: - Run entirely locally (offline) without external internet APIs. - Decouple the Sensor Ingestion logic from the core State Machine. - Open a local network port (e.g., UDP 8000) to listen for incoming JSON sensor payloads.
boundaries.must_not_do: - DO NOT attempt to read local laptop hardware sensors. - DO NOT integrate accelerometer data to calculate continuous speed (velocity). Use it strictly for binary stationary detection.

project_specification.technology_stack: - Language: Python 3.10+ - Map Parsing: `osmnx` - Graph Routing: `networkx` - Networking: `socket` (UDP/TCP) or `FastAPI` (for HTTP POST ingestion)

architecture.data_structures:
1. `Milestone`: Represents a physical OSM node. - `id` (str): Node ID. - `type` (str): "stop_sign", "traffic_light", etc. - `distance_from_prior` (float): Meters.
2. `NavState`: Tracks global movement. - `current_milestone_index` (int): Active route pointer. - `estimated_speed` (float): System speed in m/s. - `accumulated_distance` (float): Meters.
3. `SensorState`: Singleton tracking remote IMU telemetry. - `raw_accel` (tuple): Latest (x, y, z) vector. - `accel_variance` (float): Rolling 1.5s variance magnitude. - `is_stationary` (bool): Binary state trigger.

business_rules.rule: **Sensor Ingestion (UDP Bridge):** Run a background thread listening on `0.0.0.0:8000`. It expects incoming JSON payloads containing `accel_x`, `accel_y`, `accel_z` (e.g., from the 'Sensor Logger' iOS/Android app). Parse this stream into the `SensorState` singleton.

**Stationary Detection Engine (SDE):** Continuously calculate the variance of the acceleration vector magnitude ($\sqrt{x^2 + y^2 + z^2}$) over a rolling 1.5-second buffer. If `accel_variance` falls below `STATIONARY_THRESHOLD` (e.g., 0.02g) for > 1 second, set `is_stationary = True`. If `is_stationary == True` AND `accumulated_distance` is within 50 meters of the next `Milestone`, AUTOMATICALLY trigger the arrival handler.

**Speed Smoothing (Moving Average):** Upon a manual or automated arrival trigger, compute $V_{true} = \frac{\text{Distance}}{T_{actual}}$. Update global speed using: $\text{estimated\_speed} = (\text{estimated\_speed} \times 0.7) + (V_{true} \times 0.3)$

step.title: Network Bridge & Sensor State
step.tasks: - Implement the `UDP Listener` on port 8000 to catch incoming JSON strings. - Implement the `SensorState` singleton and the rolling variance buffer. - Log incoming telemetry to the console to verify phone-to-laptop connectivity.

step.title: State Machine & Dead Reckoning
step.tasks: - Implement `Milestone` and `NavState`. - Create a mock route of 5 milestones. - Write the ticker loop that increments `accumulated_distance`.

step.title: The Auto-Snap Integration
step.tasks: - Write the listener logic: If `is_stationary` goes high while in range of a node, calculate $V_{true}$, apply speed smoothing, advance the milestone index, and output a "NODE SNAPPED" success log.
